examples.title: Minimax Algorithm
examples.description: The fundamental algorithm for deterministic, two-player, zero-sum games with perfect information.
examples.code:
function minimax(node, depth, maximizingPlayer)
 if depth == 0 or node is terminal
 return heuristic_value(node)
 if maximizingPlayer
 value = -∞
 for each child of node
 value = max(value, minimax(child, depth-1, FALSE))
 return value
 else
 value = +∞
 for each child of node
 value = min(value, minimax(child, depth-1, TRUE))
 return value
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the minimax algorithm, which assumes that both players play optimally to maximize their own advantage.

examples.title: Minimax Implementation in Python
examples.description: A Python implementation of the minimax algorithm.
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the minimax algorithm, using a recursive approach to traverse the game tree.

examples.title: Alpha-Beta Pruning Implementation in C
examples.description: A C implementation of the alpha-beta pruning algorithm.
examples.explanation: This C code implements the alpha-beta pruning algorithm, which optimizes the minimax algorithm by eliminating branches that cannot affect the final decision.
